USD.catalyst: The U.S. dollar rally in July 2026 is being sustained by a hawkish Federal Reserve stance following the appointment of Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair, which has driven up real yields and prompted global pension funds to unwind FX hedges. Market participants are pricing in further rate hikes as gold remains pressured near the $4,000 level and Middle East tensions fan inflation fears, particularly following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The greenback maintains a structural advantage through a +275bp carry spread over the yen, with the Fed funds rate currently at 3.75% while the market awaits further testimony from Chair Warsh.

sources.title: Global pension funds pull back on FX hedges as US dollar woes ease - Forex Factory

GBP.catalyst: Sterling rose 1% on July 16 following reports that Shabana Mahmood will serve as finance minister, providing a fiscal catalyst that complemented stronger-than-expected UK GDP data. The economy grew 0.1% in May with upward revisions to April, prompting Pantheon Macroeconomics to upgrade growth forecasts despite the risk of higher oil prices. BoE 3.75%.

Pound Sterling: UK Economy Defies Higher Oil Prices, Says Pantheon - Exchange Rates UK
Dollar jumps on renewed Middle East attacks, Hormuz closure - CNBC

EUR.catalyst: The Euro remains pressured below the 1.1400 level as the European Central Bank prepares for its July 23 policy meeting, with rising oil prices returning to the top of the Governing Council's worry list. While Vice Chair Philip Jefferson has highlighted the challenges of navigating economic shocks, the currency faces headwinds from a widening policy divergence as the ECB contends with energy-driven inflation risks. Technical resistance near the 200-SMA on the H4 chart has capped recent recovery attempts, leaving the single currency range-bound against a dominant dollar.

sources.title: Back to square one: Five questions for the ECB - Reuters
Pound Sterling: UK Growth Beats Expectations As Economists Upgrade GDP Outlook - Exchange Rates UK

JPY.catalyst: The Japanese Yen led G10 gains after Finance Minister Katayama unexpectedly urged the Government Pension Investment Fund (GPIF) and domestic households to increase investments in local financial assets during a July press conference. Despite this verbal intervention, USD/JPY remains elevated at 162.439, well above the critical 160.00 threshold, as Katayama reaffirmed that specific monetary policy settings remain the sole prerogative of the Bank of Japan. The currency continues to struggle against a massive +275bp carry disadvantage, even as a 4.25% drop in the Nikkei on July 17 sparked some safe-haven flows.

AUD.catalyst: Fed 3.75%. dollar's safe-haven appeal. While the currency found temporary support from risk-on flows and stabilization above the 20-day EMA, the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has introduced a volatility premium that offsets domestic optimism. Traders are currently balancing positive technical momentum against the broader geopolitical shift that favors the greenback in the high-inflation environment of mid-2026.

sources.title: Dollar jumps on renewed Middle East attacks, Hormuz closure - CNBC

CAD.catalyst: The Canadian Dollar is exhibiting a severe cross-asset correlation breakdown, trading at 1.40350 against the USD despite WTI crude oil rising to $79.20. BoC 2.25%. While the currency held near a four-week high on July 16 due to narrowing yield spreads, it remains vulnerable to renewed dollar strength as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East outweigh the impact of the marginal rise in oil prices.

sources.title: USDCAD Outlook Canadian Dollar Shows Weakness Amid New Geopolitical Tension Updates
Canadian dollar holds near four-week high as yield spreads narrow

CHF.catalyst: The Swiss Franc remains anchored by the Swiss National Bank's assessment that monetary conditions are appropriate, with recent meeting minutes indicating that while inflation risks have risen, there is no immediate need for action. SNB 0.00%. Retail sentiment is currently extremely stretched in GBP/CHF, with 91% of retail traders holding short positions in direct opposition to institutional long positioning.

NZD.catalyst: RBNZ 2.5%. This hawkish trajectory is reinforced by a +250bp carry premium over the Swiss Franc and positive technical signals as NZD/JPY completes a corrective pullback. While domestic news was dominated by Will Jordan’s record-breaking 50th test try in the Nations Championship on July 11, the currency's market direction is fundamentally driven by the central bank's hiking cycle and a restrictive monetary policy stance.

sources.title: Jordan achieves a try-scoring record as New Zealand beats Italy 47-17 in the Nations Championship - AP News

NOK.catalyst: The Norwegian Krone is currently trading in a neutral range as it lacks specific domestic catalysts from the Norges Bank or significant local data releases this week. While the currency typically tracks global energy prices, the broader G10 focus on Middle East geopolitical tensions and the U.S. dollar's dominance has left the Krone range-bound. Market participants are awaiting fresh inflation data or central bank commentary to break the current consolidation pattern.

SEK.catalyst: The Swedish Krona remains largely range-bound against its G10 peers pending fresh macroeconomic catalysts from the Riksbank. In the absence of specific policy announcements or data surprises in the July 17 session, the currency is tracking broader European sentiment and global risk appetite. Traders are maintaining a neutral stance as the Krona lacks the clear yield or fiscal drivers currently propelling the Dollar and Sterling.
